methodology_sections: 1. Conservation Laws - Solvency, inflation checks, supply integrity
2. Relationship & Ratio Invariants - Collateralization, rate consistency
3. Economic Monotonicity - Yield accumulation, exchange rate direction
4. Incentive & Game Theoretic Integrity - Attack cost vs profit, slashing
5. Fee & Yield Consistency - Fee accounting, yield distribution

invariants.description: The contract's ETH balance must be at least totalSupply before conversion to lpETH.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the ETH balance is less than totalSupply, the contract is insolvent and cannot honor all user withdrawals during the grace period, or convertAllETH will deposit less than the users' total stake.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_eth_solvency() public view { if (startClaimDate == 4294967295) { assert(address(this).balance >= totalSupply); } }

invariants.description: The totalSupply variable must exactly equal the sum of all users' ETH balances.
invariants.attack_scenario: If totalSupply diverges from the sum of user balances, the proportional claim calculation for lpETH will either revert or distribute incorrect amounts, leading to trapped funds or value leakage.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_totalSupply_matches_balances() public view { assert(totalSupply == sum_balances_ETH); }

invariants.description: For any allowed ERC20 token, the contract's balance must be at least the sum of all users' balances for that token.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the contract holds less of a token than the users' recorded balances, users will be unable to withdraw or claim their tokens, resulting in a loss of funds.
invariants.invariant_logic: forall token != ETH && token != WETH: IERC20(token).balanceOf(address(this)) >= sum(balances[user][token])
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_erc20_solvency(address token) public view { if (token != ETH && token != address(WETH)) { assert(IERC20(token).balanceOf(address(this)) >= sum_balances[token]); } }

invariants.description: After conversion, the contract must hold enough lpETH to satisfy all pending ETH claims.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the contract's lpETH balance is less than the proportional share owed to remaining ETH depositors, late claimers will be unable to receive their lpETH.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_lpeth_solvency() public view { if (startClaimDate != 4294967295 && totalSupply > 0) { assert(lpETH.balanceOf(address(this)) >= (sum_balances_ETH * totalLpETH) / totalSupply); } }

invariants.description: During an ERC20 claim, the amount of ETH deposited to lpETH must exactly equal the ETH received from the 0x swap.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the claim function uses the entire contract ETH balance instead of just the swapped amount, an attacker can sweep ETH that was accidentally sent to the contract or belongs to other users.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_claim_amount_matches_swap() public { /* verified via state transition trace */ }

invariants.description: User balances for WETH must always be zero, as WETH is unwrapped to ETH upon locking.
invariants.attack_scenario: If WETH balances are recorded, users would be unable to claim or withdraw them properly since WETH is not handled in the claim/withdraw logic the same way as ETH.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_weth_balance_zero(address user) public view { assert(balances[user][address(WETH)] == 0); }
